title: Cooling Tower Placement for Noise and Contamination
summary: Cooling towers should be located at least 30 m from the building to reduce noise and prevent discharge air/moisture from affecting windows, facade, or outdoor air intakes.
evidence_quote: If the cooling tower or water-cooled condenser is located at ground level, it should be at least 30 m away from the building to reduce tower noise in the building, and to keep discharge air and moisture carryover from fogging the building’s windows and discoloring the building facade or from contaminating outdoor air being introduced into the building.

title: Equipment Room Ceiling Height Requirement
summary: Equipment rooms generally require clear ceiling heights ranging from 3 to 5 m, depending on equipment sizes and distribution complexity.
evidence_quote: Equipment rooms generally require clear ceiling height ranging from 3 to 5 m, depending on equipment sizes and the complexity of air and/or water distribution.

title: Primary Equipment Room Flood Zone Restriction
summary: Primary equipment rooms should not be located below ground or at ground level in designated flood zones due to flooding risk.
evidence_quote: Note that primary equipment rooms should not be located below ground or even at ground level if the project is to be located in a designated flood zone area, due to the potential of the equipment room being flooded.

title: Fan Room Access for Equipment Replacement
summary: Fan rooms must have space for removing fans, shafts, coils, and filters, considering installation, replacement, and maintenance.
evidence_quote: The room must have space for removing the fan(s), shaft(s), coils, and filters. Installation, replacement, and maintenance of this equipment should be considered when locating and arranging the room.

title: Vertical Shaft Aspect Ratio Recommendation
summary: Duct shafts with an aspect ratio of 2:1 to 4:1 are easier to develop than large square shafts and facilitate transitions from fan rooms.
evidence_quote: In general, duct shafts with an aspect ratio of 2:1 to 4:1 are easier to develop than large square shafts. The rectangular shape also facilitates transition from the equipment in the fan rooms to the shafts.

title: Rooftop Equipment Access Requirement
summary: Access to rooftop equipment should be by means of, at a minimum, a ship’s ladder, not a vertical ladder, considering technicians carrying replacement filters.
evidence_quote: Access to rooftop equipment should be by means of, at a minimum, a ship’s ladder and not by a vertical ladder, taking into account a technician carrying boxes of replacement filters.

title: Return Air Plenum Temperature Differential
summary: The air temperature in a return air plenum directly below a roof deck is usually 2 to 3 K higher during air-conditioning season than in a ducted return.
evidence_quote: The air temperature in a return air plenum directly below a roof deck is usually higher by 2 to 3 K during the air-conditioning season than in a ducted return.

title: Enhanced Particulate Filtration for Security
summary: Enhanced particulate filtration for air-handling systems to MERV 14 to 16 filters reduces circulation of dangerous substances and improves indoor air quality.
evidence_quote: Enhanced particulate filtration for air-handling systems to the level of MERV 14 to 16 filters not only tends to reduce circulation of dangerous substances (e.g., anthrax), but also provides better indoor air quality (IAQ).

title: Safety Valve Minimum Differential Pressure
summary: A minimum differential of 35 kPa or 10% of inlet pressure (whichever is greater) is recommended for safety valve set pressure to allow proper cycling.
evidence_quote: A minimum differential of 35 kPa or 10% of inlet pressure (whichever is greater) is recommended.

title: Safety Valve Testing Frequency
summary: Under normal conditions, safety valves with working pressure under 2.8 MPa should be tested manually once per month and pressure-tested once each year.
evidence_quote: Under normal conditions, safety valves with a working pressure under 2.8 MPa should be tested manually once per month and pressure-tested once each year.
